project.name: ble-calculator-desktop
project.description: Cross-platform Electron calculator sync app backed by a Rust napi-rs core.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: BLE peripheral module providing platform abstraction and configuration for cross-platform BLE advertising. Defines PeripheralConfig for service/characteristic UUIDs and BlePeripheral trait with platform-specific implementations.
nodes.languageNotes: Rust module with conditional compilation for different platforms, using trait-based abstraction for BLE peripheral operations.

nodes.name: PeripheralConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration struct for BLE peripheral advertising, defining service UUID, RX/TX characteristic UUIDs, and local name format for join/room beacon modes.

nodes.name: BlePeripheral
nodes.summary: Trait defining platform-agnostic BLE peripheral interface with methods for advertising control, inbound data handling, notifications, and status queries.

nodes.name: new_peripheral
nodes.summary: Factory function that instantiates the appropriate platform-specific BLE peripheral implementation (macOS, Linux, Windows, or stub) based on compile-time target.

nodes.name: peripheral_linux.rs
nodes.summary: Linux BLE peripheral implementation using BlueZ via bluetooth crate. Provides async GATT server with RX characteristic for write requests and TX characteristic for notifications, running on a dedicated tokio runtime thread.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses bluetooth crate for BlueZ integration with async/await patterns, running on a dedicated current-thread tokio runtime for BLE operations.

nodes.name: LinuxPeripheral
nodes.summary: Linux implementation of BlePeripheral trait using BlueZ, managing advertising state, inbound data queue, and notification channel with async worker thread.

nodes.name: start_session
nodes.summary: Creates and starts the BlueZ GATT server session with service and characteristics, setting up write handler for RX characteristic and notify handler for TX characteristic subscription.

nodes.name: run_worker
nodes.summary: Async worker loop processing Start/Notify/Stop commands for BlueZ peripheral, managing advertising lifecycle and notification delivery.

nodes.name: peripheral_macos.rs
nodes.summary: macOS BLE peripheral implementation using CoreBluetooth via objc2 framework. Provides GATT server with RX/TX characteristics, dispatch queue integration, and overflow-safe notification queuing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses objc2 for type-safe Objective-C interop with CoreBluetooth, dispatch queues for thread safety, and AssertSend wrapper for Send bounds.

nodes.name: MacosPeripheral
nodes.summary: macOS implementation of BlePeripheral using CoreBluetooth's CBPeripheralManager, with dispatch queue integration and overflow-safe outbound queue management.

nodes.name: drain_outbound
nodes.summary: Dispatches queued frames to subscribed central via CBCharacteristic notify, respecting max update length and handling unsubscribe/disconnect errors.

nodes.name: apply_pending
nodes.summary: Applies pending advertising configuration by creating GATT service with RX/TX characteristics and starting advertising with local name and service UUIDs.

nodes.name: peripheral_stub.rs
nodes.summary: Stub BLE peripheral implementation for unsupported platforms, returning errors for all operations. Used as fallback when platform-specific backend is not available.

nodes.name: StubPeripheral
nodes.summary: No-op implementation of BlePeripheral that returns errors for all operations, used on platforms without BLE peripheral support.

nodes.name: peripheral_windows.rs
nodes.summary: Windows BLE peripheral implementation using Windows.Networking.Bluetooth namespace via windows-rs crate. Provides GATT server with RX/TX characteristics and handles write requests from centrals.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses windows-rs crate for Windows API interop, with async/await patterns for GATT operations and typed event handlers.

nodes.name: WindowsPeripheral
nodes.summary: Windows implementation of BlePeripheral using Windows BLE APIs, managing GATT service provider and active session for notification delivery.

nodes.name: build_session
nodes.summary: Creates Windows GATT service provider with custom service and RX/TX characteristics, setting up write handler for inbound data and tracking subscribed clients.

nodes.name: ble_central.rs
nodes.summary: BLE central role implementation for scanning and connecting to peripherals. Manages adapter, active peripheral connection, and notification stream using blesc crate for async BLE operations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses blesc crate with tokio runtime for async BLE operations, maintaining global singleton state for adapter and connection.

nodes.name: scan
nodes.summary: Scans for BLE peripherals advertising the calculator service UUID for a specified duration, returning discovered peripherals with their properties.

nodes.name: connect
nodes.summary: Connects to a peripheral by address, discovers services, finds calculator RX/TX characteristics, subscribes to TX notifications, and stores the active connection.

nodes.name: write_frames
nodes.summary: Writes calculation result frames to the connected peripheral's RX characteristic, handling connection state verification and write type selection.

nodes.name: lib.rs
nodes.summary: Main native module implementing cross-device calculator sync with BLE transport, SQLite persistence, keychain identity, and JWS/JWE cryptographic signing. Entry point for napi-rs exports to JavaScript/TypeScript layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Large module (2342 lines) combining business logic, crypto operations, BLE orchestration, persistence, and expression evaluation as napi-rs exports.

nodes.name: get_state
nodes.summary: Returns current application state including local device ID, session role, connected peers, rooms, and calculation history, refreshing from BLE and storage before returning.

nodes.name: create_room
nodes.summary: Creates a new calculator room with unique ID, sets host role, clears existing peers/rooms, and starts BLE advertising with room beacon for guests to discover.

nodes.name: join_room
nodes.summary: Joins an existing room discovered via BLE scan, sets guest role with peripheral BLE advertising, and initiates connection to the room host.

nodes.name: connect_guest
nodes.summary: As host, connects to a discovered guest peer via BLE central, marks peer as connected, and returns updated state with the new connection.

nodes.name: submit_calculation
nodes.summary: Evaluates arithmetic expression, creates signed calculation event using local identity, persists to storage, and delivers via BLE to guest or host depending on session role.

nodes.name: process_incoming_ble
nodes.summary: Collects inbound frames from BLE peripheral or central notifications, reassembles chunks, verifies signatures, and merges verified events into history with peer tracking.

nodes.name: ingest_ble_frames
nodes.summary: Reassembles BLE transport chunks into complete payloads, verifies signature and holder binding, and returns validated calculation entries with warning tracking.

nodes.name: deliver_event_to_guest
nodes.summary: As host, delivers calculation event to connected guest via BLE central write, chunking payload to fit MTU and refreshing connection state on error.

nodes.name: deliver_event_to_host
nodes.summary: As guest, delivers calculation event to host via BLE peripheral notify, respecting platform-specific max frame length and queuing overflow detection.

nodes.name: frame_event_for_ble
nodes.summary: Chunks signed calculation event payload into BLE transport frames sized for the MTU budget, returning serialized frame array with overhead accounting.

nodes.name: sign_calculation_event
nodes.summary: Creates JWS signature for calculation event with embedded public key in protected header, returning flattened JWS envelope with protected/payload/signature bas64-encoded.

nodes.name: verify_received_calculation_event
nodes.summary: Verifies received JWS envelope by extracting embedded public key, validating signature, and ensuring holder key binding matches the origin device ID.

nodes.name: load_or_create_identity
nodes.summary: Loads existing Ed25519 signing key from platform keychain or generates new one, returning identity with keychain backing status and public key fingerprint.

nodes.name: calculate_expression
nodes.summary: Parses and evaluates arithmetic expression with operator precedence, returning result scaled to 8 decimal places or error for invalid expressions.

nodes.name: parse_expression
nodes.summary: Tokenizes and parses arithmetic expression using shunting-yard algorithm for operator precedence, evaluating to final numeric result.

nodes.name: scan_ble_join_requests
nodes.summary: Scans for BLE join request advertisements matching the optional room filter, returning discovered peers who want to join the host's room.

nodes.name: scan_ble_rooms
nodes.summary: Scans for BLE room beacon advertisements, returning discovered host rooms that guests can join.

nodes.name: parse_local_name_advertisement
nodes.summary: Parses BLE local name in format 'EvolveCalc:KIND:ROOM_ID:LABEL' to extract advertisement payload fields for join/room beacon modes.

nodes.name: load_history
nodes.summary: Loads calculation history from SQLite database with optional limit, querying all columns and converting to CalculationEntry structs with warning tracking.

nodes.name: persist_calculation
nodes.summary: Persists local calculation entry to SQLite in calculations table, serializing full event envelope as JSON and updating outbox sync status.

nodes.name: chunk_payload_sized
nodes.summary: Splits payload into fixed-size chunks with BleTransportChunk wrapper containing message ID, index, total count, and base64-encoded chunk data.

nodes.name: reassemble_chunks
nodes.summary: Reassembles BLE transport chunks into complete payload, validating sequence, message ID consistency, and decoding base64 chunk data.

nodes.name: browser-calculator.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory browser implementation of the calculator API for development/testing. Provides BLE session simulation, room management, peer discovery, and calculation submission without native dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Factory function returning an API object with methods matching the native interface. Uses immutable state cloning pattern with cloneState helper.

nodes.name: createBrowserCalculatorApi
nodes.summary: Factory function creating a mock calculator API implementation. Returns an object with getState, createRoom, startScanning, connectGuest, scanRooms, joinRoom, startAdvertising, acceptHostConnection, resetBleSession, submitCalculation, and pollState methods for browser-based testing.

nodes.name: log-store.ts
nodes.summary: Immutable log storage implementation using React's external store pattern. Provides a LogStore class with push, clear, toText methods and subscribe/getSnapshot for React integration via useSyncExternalStore.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements React's useSyncExternalStore API with subscribe/getSnapshot for reactive log updates.

nodes.name: LogStore
nodes.summary: Immutable log store class implementing React external store pattern. Stores log entries with auto-incrementing IDs and provides push, clear, toText, emit methods plus subscribe/getSnapshot for React integration.

nodes.name: logging-calculator-api.ts
nodes.summary: Logging middleware wrapper for calculator API calls. Wraps any calculator API implementation to log method invocations, results, timing, diagnostics, and errors to the log store for debugging.
nodes.languageNotes: Higher-order factory pattern wrapping API methods with logging instrumentation.

nodes.name: summarizeState
nodes.summary: Formats calculator state into a compact multi-line summary string showing peer count, room count, connection status, and pending actions for logging.

nodes.name: describeArgs
nodes.summary: Formats function arguments into a concise string representation with value shortening for logging brevity.

nodes.name: logStartupEnvironment
nodes.summary: Logs application startup environment details including native bridge availability, platform context, and initial diagnostics to the log store.

nodes.name: createLoggingCalculatorApi
nodes.summary: Factory creating a logging wrapper around any calculator API implementation. Intercepts all API calls to log invocation, timing, results, state changes, and errors before delegating to the underlying API.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Main React renderer entry point implementing the calculator UI with BLE networking. Provides host/guest role selection, room discovery, peer connection management, calculation input with history, and a debug log console drawer.
nodes.languageNotes: Single file React application with 20 component functions and extensive state management. No separate component files.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component managing calculator UI state, BLE networking, role switching, and drawer navigation. Integrates calculator API with polling, handles expression input, maintains connection history, and orchestrates all UI interactions.

nodes.name: LogConsole
nodes.summary: Drawer component displaying debug logs from the log store. Uses useSyncExternalStore for reactive updates, provides copy-to-clipboard functionality with fallback textarea selection, and clear logs action.

nodes.name: DiscoveryPane
nodes.summary: Tab panel showing available BLE peers (for host) and rooms (for guest) with connection actions. Filters items based on UI role, displays RSSI signal strength, connection status, and provides connect/join buttons.

nodes.name: ConnectionCard
nodes.summary: Card component displaying connected peer info with trust badge, role label, last sync timestamp, and disconnect button. Shows connection status and provides connection termination action.

nodes.name: HostFields
nodes.summary: Form fields for host role including room name input, create room button, find guests scanning action, and connection status. Shows pending states and disables actions appropriately during BLE operations.

nodes.name: GuestFields
nodes.summary: Form fields for guest role including room code input, find hosts scanning action, join by code button, and connection status. Shows pending states and disables actions appropriately during BLE operations.

nodes.name: RoleSwitchModal
nodes.summary: Confirmation dialog shown when switching between host and guest roles. Warns about BLE session reset, requires explicit confirmation before proceeding with role change.

nodes.name: SessionFacts
nodes.summary: Status footer component displaying current session facts including role, room ID, peer connection status, and pending action. Provides visual feedback on current network state.

nodes.name: expression.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the expression calculator utility. Validates arithmetic expression parsing and calculation correctness including basic operations and edge cases.

nodes.name: expression.ts
nodes.summary: Safe arithmetic expression evaluator supporting addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and modulo operations. Parses infix expressions, validates input sanitization, and returns formatted decimal results.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stack-based evaluation with operator precedence. No eval() - safe parsing with regex validation.

nodes.name: calculateExpression
nodes.summary: Parses and evaluates arithmetic expressions with operators +, -, *, /, %. Sanitizes input, validates format, applies operator precedence, and returns formatted decimal result or throws on error.

nodes.name: applyOp
nodes.summary: Applies a single arithmetic operation to two number values. Handles division by zero, modulo by zero, and returns the result pushed back onto the values stack.

nodes.name: main.ts
nodes.summary: Electron main process entry point that registers IPC handlers for calculator operations and creates the application window with sandbox configuration.

nodes.name: native-calculator.ts
nodes.summary: Loads the native Rust napi-rs BLE calculator module from multiple candidate paths with runtime type validation, falling back to a mock implementation for development environments.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses createRequire to load native .node modules with graceful fallback to mock API for environments without the native extension.

nodes.name: preload.ts
nodes.summary: Electron preload script that exposes the calculator API to the renderer process via contextBridge, mapping all IPC channel invocations.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld to safely expose IPC-bound API without direct Node.js access in renderer.

nodes.name: global.d.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript global declaration that extends the Window interface with the calculator API exposed by the preload script.

nodes.name: calculator-api.ts
nodes.summary: Shared TypeScript types and IPC channel names defining the calculator API contract between Electron main and renderer processes.

nodes.name: registerCalculatorIpc
nodes.summary: Registers IPC handlers for all calculator API channels, bridging renderer requests to the native calculator implementation.

nodes.name: createWindow
nodes.summary: Creates and configures the Electron BrowserWindow with sandbox security, loading either dev server or production bundle.

nodes.name: getCalculatorApi
nodes.summary: Singleton accessor that returns the cached native API, loading it on first use with fallback to mock implementation.

nodes.name: loadNativeApi
nodes.summary: Attempts to load the native Rust module from multiple candidate paths with runtime interface validation.

nodes.name: createMockCalculatorApi
nodes.summary: Creates a complete mock implementation of the calculator API with in-memory state for development and testing without the native module.

nodes.name: cloneState
nodes.summary: Deep clones the RoomState using JSON serialization to prevent state mutation across API calls.

nodes.name: release.yml
nodes.summary: GitHub Actions workflow that builds native installers for macOS, Linux, and Windows platforms in parallel and publishes GitHub releases on version tags.
nodes.languageNotes: Matrix build strategy with platform-specific Rust compilation, using separate native runners for each target OS to produce genuine native modules.

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Matrix build job that compiles and packages native installers for macOS (arm64+x64), Linux (x64), and Windows (x64) on their respective OS runners.

nodes.name: release
nodes.summary: Publishes GitHub release with all installer artifacts, triggered by version tags or manual workflow dispatch.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Main project documentation covering the BLE Calculator Desktop Electron app architecture, commands, visual UI pass workflow, and native Rust module status with BLE sync capabilities.

nodes.name: electron-builder.yml
nodes.summary: Electron Builder configuration for packaging the Evolve Calc desktop app, defining app metadata, file inclusion patterns, asar unpack rules, and platform-specific targets (DMG for macOS, NSIS for Windows, AppImage/deb for Linux) with Bluetooth usage descriptions.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-platform packaging config with platform-specific overrides and macOS Bluetooth permission strings required for CoreBluetooth BLE operations.

nodes.name: evolve-calculator-theme.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive UI theme specification extracting the Evolve brand identity (warm colors, condensed type, cream/charcoal palette) and applying it to a desktop calculator layout with detailed CSS tokens, component sketches, and interaction guidelines.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point for the Electron renderer process with strict Content Security Policy, mounting the React app root and loading the main renderer TypeScript module.
nodes.languageNotes: Restrictive CSP limits sources to self and the Vite dev server at 127.0.0.1:5173, allowing only inline styles for HMR.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM package configuration for the BLE Calculator Desktop app, defining dependencies (React, lucide-react), dev tooling (TypeScript, Vite, Vitest, Electron), and build scripts for development, native module compilation, testing, and cross-platform packaging.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration with strict mode enabled, targeting ES2022 with NodeNext module resolution, React JSX transform, and indexed access safety for the Electron + React desktop application.

nodes.name: calculator-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive architecture document covering the Electron+React+Rust calculator app, including runtime architecture, UI behavior, native module boundaries, BLE roles, security boundaries, end-to-end user flows, and planned development steps.
nodes.languageNotes: 43-section markdown with extensive diagrams, tables, and cross-references to source files.

nodes.name: developer-onboarding.md
nodes.summary: Concise developer onboarding guide providing the shortest path for new developers to understand the calculator app, with read-first document pointers, main code paths, local development commands, validation steps, and common development tasks.

nodes.name: runtime-native-and-security.md
nodes.summary: Technical deep-dive on Electron security settings, IPC channels, native adapter loading, Rust scaffold, cross-platform BLE peripheral backends (macOS CoreBluetooth, Linux BlueZ, Windows GattServiceProvider), secure storage planning, and outstanding security work.
nodes.languageNotes: 27 sections covering platform-specific BLE backends and security boundary planning.

nodes.name: ui-layout-and-visual-pass.md
nodes.summary: UI layout documentation explaining the grid-based layout model, fixed calculator dimensions (420px x 620px), overflow rules, component responsibilities, browser mock for visual review, and automated screenshot testing via Chrome DevTools Protocol.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Configures the Understand-Anything knowledge graph tool by excluding generated artifacts, build outputs, and test files from analysis.

nodes.name: report.json
nodes.summary: Test artifact containing visual regression test results from the browser-based UI pass verification script.

nodes.name: build.rs
nodes.summary: Rust build script that invokes napi-build to configure the napi-rs native module compilation.

nodes.name: Cargo.toml
nodes.summary: Rust package configuration defining the napi-rs native module with platform-specific BLE dependencies (Core Bluetooth on macOS, WinRT on Windows, BlueZ on Linux).
nodes.languageNotes: Conditional dependencies per target OS (macos/windows/linux) for platform-specific BLE implementations.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the Rust napi-rs module scope, including current scaffold (mock BLE data) and planned native responsibilities (BLE flows, chunking, keychain, SQLite, cryptography).

nodes.name: eslint.config.mjs
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration enabling TypeScript, React Hooks rules, and consistent-type-imports enforcement across the codebase.

nodes.name: browser-visual-ui-pass.mjs
nodes.summary: Visual regression testing script that launches Chrome with CDP, captures screenshots, applies scenario scripts, and computes element overlap metrics.

nodes.name: build-mac-arm64.command
nodes.summary: Shell script that builds the native Rust module for macOS arm64 target using cargo, then restores the .node artifact to the project root.

nodes.name: build-native-optional.mjs
nodes.summary: Conditional native module build script that only runs cargo build if the .node artifact is missing, skipping build for prebuilt artifacts.

nodes.name: build-native.mjs
nodes.summary: Native module build script that invokes cargo with napi-rs to compile the Rust core into a Node.js addon.

nodes.name: next-version.mjs
nodes.summary: Version bumping utility that queries GitHub for the latest release tag and prints the next development version number.

nodes.name: package-platforms.mjs
nodes.summary: Cross-platform packaging orchestrator that builds native modules for multiple targets, runs electron-builder, and manages Rust toolchain installation per target.

nodes.name: release-all.bat
nodes.summary: Windows batch script that builds the native module, installs dependencies, and runs the cross-platform packaging script for all platforms.

nodes.name: release-all.command
nodes.summary: macOS command script that builds the native module, installs dependencies, and runs the cross-platform packaging script for all platforms.

nodes.name: visual-ui-pass.mjs
nodes.summary: Electron-based visual UI verification script that waits for the dev server, applies scenario scripts, and captures screenshots for regression testing.

nodes.name: visual-ui-screenshot-app.cjs
nodes.summary: Electron app launcher for visual testing that loads the renderer, executes scenario scripts via CDP, captures screenshots, and computes element overlap metrics.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: HTML entry point for the renderer process with strict CSP and a root div where React mounts.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the renderer UI defining layout, typography, and component styles.

nodes.name: vite.config.mts
nodes.summary: Vite configuration for the renderer dev server with React plugin, custom root, and strict port binding.

nodes.name: vitest.config.mts
nodes.summary: Vitest configuration defining test file patterns and excluding build artifacts from the test run.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main test orchestrator that launches Chrome, connects via CDP, runs visual scenarios, captures screenshots, and writes overlap reports.

nodes.name: scenarioScript
nodes.summary: Constructs a CDP script that evaluates a user action (type or click) on a DOM element during visual testing.

nodes.name: metricsScript
nodes.summary: Constructs a CDP script that queries element positions and sizes to compute overlap metrics for visual regression detection.

nodes.name: getOverlaps
nodes.summary: Computes overlapping areas between rectangle pairs to detect UI element collisions.

nodes.name: waitForLoad
nodes.summary: Waits for a page load event via CDP with timeout and cleanup support.

nodes.name: waitForHttp
nodes.summary: Polls an HTTP URL until it responds successfully or times out.

nodes.name: fetchText
nodes.summary: Performs an HTTP GET request using Node's http module and returns the response body as text.

nodes.name: CdpClient
nodes.summary: WebSocket-based Chrome DevTools Protocol client that sends commands, handles responses via promises, and emits event notifications.

nodes.name: latestTag
nodes.summary: Queries GitHub for all tags, filters for semver format, sorts them, and returns the latest version tag.

nodes.name: buildNativeForTarget
nodes.summary: Builds the Rust native module for a specific target triple, ensuring the Rust target is installed first.

nodes.name: expandTargets
nodes.summary: Expands shorthand target names (macos, windows, linux) into full target triples for each architecture.

nodes.name: currentTargetKey
nodes.summary: Detects the current host platform and architecture to return the corresponding target triple key.

nodes.name: ensureRustTargetInstalled
nodes.summary: Checks if a Rust target is installed via rustup and adds it if missing.

nodes.name: buildEnvWithRustupCargo
nodes.summary: Builds an environment object for spawning processes, adding the rustup cargo bin directory to PATH for native module builds.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Spawns a command synchronously and throws an error if the exit code is non-zero.

nodes.name: stashNativeArtifacts
nodes.summary: Moves compiled .node artifacts to a staging directory to prevent them from being overwritten during cross-platform builds.

nodes.name: printPlan
nodes.summary: Prints a formatted summary of the build plan including targets and Electron platform groups.

nodes.name: waitForServer
nodes.summary: Polls a server URL until it becomes reachable, with timeout and retry logic.

nodes.name: canReach
nodes.summary: Attempts an HTTP GET request with a short timeout to check if a URL is reachable.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Electron app launcher that creates a window, loads the renderer, executes scenario scripts, captures screenshots, and saves overlap reports.

nodes.name: applyScenarioScript
nodes.summary: Constructs a script string that applies a user action (type or click) to a DOM element for scenario testing.

nodes.name: metricsScript
nodes.summary: Constructs a script that queries element metrics to compute overlaps for visual regression detection.

nodes.name: getOverlaps
nodes.summary: Computes overlapping rectangle areas to detect UI element collisions.

nodes.name: take_notifications
nodes.summary: Drains guest-to-host notification frames received since the last call. Non-blocking poller that returns buffered frames from the OS stack. Each item is a raw framed transport chunk for reassembly.

layers.name: Native Service Layer
layers.description: Rust napi-rs native module implementing BLE transport, cross-platform peripheral support (macOS CoreBluetooth, Linux BlueZ, Windows GattServiceProvider), cryptographic signing with Ed25519/JWS, SQLite persistence, and expression evaluation.

layers.name: Application Bridge Layer
layers.description: Electron main process, IPC handlers, and preload script providing the bridge between the Rust native module and the React renderer process with sandbox security.

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React renderer components including the main calculator app, log store with React external store pattern, logging middleware, browser-based test double implementation, styles, and HTML entry points.

layers.name: Shared Types Layer
layers.description: TypeScript type definitions and API contracts shared between Electron processes, including calculator API types, IPC channel names, and expression evaluation utilities.

layers.name: Build Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Build scripts, cross-platform packaging orchestrators, release automation, and native module compilation scripts for macOS, Windows, and Linux targets.

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Project configuration files for NPM package, TypeScript compiler, Vite dev server, Vitest testing, Rust Cargo crate, Electron Builder packaging, and ESLint linting.

layers.name: CI/CD Layer
layers.description: GitHub Actions workflow for cross-platform native module compilation, Electron app packaging, and release automation across macOS, Linux, and Windows targets.

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project documentation including README, architecture deep-dive, developer onboarding guide, security and runtime details, UI layout specifications, Evolve theme design, and Rust native module README.

layers.name: Project Tools Layer
layers.description: Tool configuration and meta files including Understand-Anything exclusions and project-specific tool configuration.

tour.title: Electron Entry Point
tour.description: The main process bootstraps the Electron application, registering IPC handlers for calculator operations and creating the sandboxed browser window. This is where the runtime architecture begins: the main process holds the native module and exposes a secure API to the renderer.
tour.languageLesson: Electron's main process runs in Node.js and creates renderer processes. The sandbox setting in webPreferences isolates the renderer from direct Node.js access, requiring IPC (Inter-Process Communication) for any privileged operations.

tour.title: Secure IPC Bridge
tour.description: The preload script runs in an isolated context between the main and renderer processes. It exposes the calculator API via contextBridge, mapping IPC channel invocations while preventing direct access to Node.js APIs. This is the security boundary: renderer processes never touch Node.js directly.
tour.languageLesson: Electron's contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld safely exposes APIs to the renderer. The preload script has access to Node.js APIs (ipcRenderer) but can only selectively expose them to the renderer, maintaining the principle of least privilege.

tour.title: API Contract
tour.description: The shared TypeScript types define the contract between Electron main and renderer processes. This file specifies all IPC channel names, request/response types, and the complete calculator API surface. Both ends of the IPC bridge depend on this single source of truth for type safety.
tour.languageLesson: Shared TypeScript types across process boundaries enable end-to-end type safety. The renderer calls window.calculator.* methods as if they were local, but the preload script transparently forwards these to IPC channels bound in the main process.

tour.title: Native Module Loader
tour.description: The native calculator loader attempts to load the Rust napi-rs module from multiple candidate paths, with runtime interface validation. If the native module is unavailable, it falls back to a mock implementation for development. This graceful fallback enables UI development before the Rust backend is built.
tour.languageLesson: Node.js native addons (.node files) are platform-specific binaries. The loader uses createRequire for ESM compatibility and checks for expected exports at runtime, throwing a clear error if the module interface doesn't match expectations.

tour.title: Rust Native Core
tour.description: The native lib.rs is the heart of the application: it implements cross-device calculator sync with BLE transport, SQLite persistence, keychain identity management, and JWS cryptographic signing. All napi-rs exports are defined here, bridging Rust functions to JavaScript calls through the native module loader.
tour.languageLesson: napi-rs is a framework for building pre-compiled Node.js addons in Rust. It serializes complex Rust types (structs, enums, Results) into JavaScript values automatically, so native functions feel like regular async JS functions to callers.

tour.title: BLE Platform Abstraction
tour.description: The BLE module defines a platform-agnostic trait for peripheral operations, with conditional compilation selecting the appropriate implementation for macOS (CoreBluetooth), Linux (BlueZ), Windows (WinRT), or a stub for unsupported platforms. This abstraction layer keeps the core business logic independent of OS-specific BLE APIs.
tour.languageLesson: Rust traits define shared behavior across platform-specific implementations. Conditional compilation (cfg_attr) selects the correct backend at compile time, so each platform binary only includes its own dependencies—no Windows APIs on macOS, no BlueZ on Windows.

tour.title: Platform Implementations
tour.description: Each platform file implements the BlePeripheral trait using the native BLE stack: peripheral_macos.rs uses CoreBluetooth via objc2, peripheral_linux.rs uses BlueZ via the bluetooth crate, and peripheral_windows.rs uses Windows GATT via windows-rs. These files handle the messy platform details so the core logic stays clean.
tour.languageLesson: Cross-platform native development often requires separate bindings for each OS. objc2 provides type-safe Objective-C interop on macOS, windows-rs generates Rust bindings from WinIDL metadata, and bluez provides async tokio integration for Linux Bluetooth.

tour.title: React UI and State
tour.description: The renderer main.tsx is a single-file React application implementing the calculator UI with host/guest role selection, room discovery, peer connection management, and calculation input. It wraps the calculator API with logging middleware, polls for state updates, and maintains the entire UI state tree in one component.
tour.languageLesson: React components in Electron can use browser APIs like fetch and Web Workers, but Node.js APIs require IPC through the preload bridge. This app uses useSyncExternalStore for reactive log updates and direct state management without a framework library.

tour.title: Utilities and Testing
tour.description: The expression.ts module provides safe arithmetic evaluation with stack-based parsing and operator precedence—no eval() for security. Paired with expression.test.ts for unit testing, and log-store.ts for React-integrated debugging, these utilities demonstrate the project's approach to safety and developer experience.
tour.languageLesson: Safe expression parsing avoids eval() by tokenizing input and using a stack-based algorithm (shunting-yard for precedence). Tests use Vitest, a Vite-native test runner that shares the same config as the dev server for consistency.

tour.title: Build and Release Pipeline
tour.description: The project uses a multi-stage build: Cargo compiles the Rust module via napi-rs, Vite bundles the React UI, and electron-builder packages everything into platform-specific installers. The release workflow orchestrates cross-platform builds across macOS, Linux, and Windows in parallel, each on its own OS runner with platform-specific Rust toolchains.
tour.languageLesson: Cross-platform Electron apps require building native modules on each target OS. Rust targets are added via rustup, cargo compiles platform-specific .node binaries, and electron-builder assembles the final app. GitHub Actions matrix builds run on separate runners for genuine native compilation.

tour.title: Architecture Documentation
tour.description: The project's documentation layer explains the complete system: calculator-architecture.md covers runtime architecture, BLE roles, and security boundaries; developer-onboarding.md provides the shortest path for new contributors; runtime-native-and-security.md documents the IPC security model; and ui-layout-and-visual-pass.md explains the fixed grid layout and screenshot testing approach.
